microsoft_ft: Drove **end-to-end program delivery** of Azure's resilience validation platform, standardizing schedules and workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and achieve **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining requirements and self-service scheduling that **reduced operational toil by 30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Coordinated **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ contract, managing task details and status updates across matrixed teams.
Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level drill program, delivering in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate by aligning multifunctional engineering teams and surfacing critical hardware defects.
Built an AI agent to automate drill planning workflows, **cutting planning cycle time 39%** and increasing program capacity by 21% — enabling the team to scale without proportional headcount growth.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.
Facilitated 11+ structured user interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the **product roadmap** to include intent-based YAML generation.
Improved data integrity and reduced documentation lookup time by **83%** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating content to an AI-powered semantic search platform.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, accelerating region launches by 28% and generating **$3M in accelerated revenue**.
Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to identify 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then built a **phased roadmap** to automate critical paths and reduce operational toil at scale.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track and visualize operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact investment areas.
amazon_robotics: Defined the **legacy OS migration strategy** and mapped dependencies across 1,200+ stations, achieving zero operational downtime during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition at scale.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across a multidisciplinary engineering team.
Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment lifecycle by 25%.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing end-to-end scoping and invoicing for 200+ monthly proposals, redesigning the CRM workflow to eliminate scheduling bottlenecks and improve on-time delivery.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google Business profile for targeted lead generation.

Identified and closed margin leaks by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, delivering data-driven pricing recommendations that improved leadership's cost-schedule performance.

tailoring_notes: 'Cost-schedule performance' (JD term) woven into pro_painters bullet 3 to mirror Applied Materials' vendor/budget language.
'Operational toil' and 'on-time delivery' used across microsoft_2022 and amazon_robotics to echo JD's 'on-time and within budget performance objectives' framing.
'Multidisciplinary engineering team' and 'matrixed teams' used in amazon_robotics and microsoft_ft to mirror JD's 'multifunctional design teams' and 'matrixed headcount' language.
'Phased roadmap' and 'product roadmap' used in microsoft_2022 and microsoft_2023 to reflect JD's 'conception and planning to implementation' lifecycle language.
'Program delivery' and 'scalable, repeatable model' in microsoft_ft mirror JD's 'strong program management and product lifecycle management' requirement.
